name: Blueberry French Toast Cobbler
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice bread diagonally into 10 to 14 slices, about 3/4 inch thick.
2. Place bread slices on a rimmed baking sheet measuring 10 x 15 x 1 inch.
3. In a medium bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, baking powder, and vanilla extract.
4. Slowly pour the egg mixture over the bread slices, turning them to coat completely.
5. Cover with plastic wrap and let stand at room temperature for 1 to 2 hours or refrigerate overnight.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F.
7. Butter a baking dish measuring 13 x 9 inches.
8. In the prepared dish, mix blueberries, sugar, cinnamon, and cornstarch until the berries are evenly coated.
9. Place bread slices, wet side up, atop the berry mixture in the dish, pressing them in tightly. If necessary, cut some slices to fit.
10. Brush the tops of the bread slices with melted butter.
11. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until the toast is golden and the berries are bubbling.
12. Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
13. Serve by transferring the toast to plates and spooning blueberry sauce over top.
